mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP and employment growth signal expanding economic activity, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation forces the Fed to tighten policy, raising discount rates applied to equity cash flows and compressing valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral despite sustained inflation acceleration above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict quarterly returns above random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust aggregate supply and demand alignment, reducing supply chain bottlenecks and supporting corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns turn negative when both INDPRO and HOUST rise simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to predict returns above random chance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a rapidly falling unemployment rate signals peak labor demand, pushing wage inflation and prompting hawkish Fed expectations, which compress equity valuations and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive when UNRATE drops below 6.0% in late-cycle periods, or the signal shows no predictive power for weekly returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect sustained consumer spending power, directly supporting revenue growth for consumer-facing S&P 500 constituents and yielding positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows no predictive power for quarterly S&P 500 returns in walk-forward testing, or the direction of returns consistently contradicts the sales trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output signals an economic slowdown or recession, eroding corporate earnings prospects and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive or unchanged despite significant industrial production declines, or the signal fails to predict monthly returns above random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index indicate an aggressive monetary tightening cycle amid persistent inflation, rapidly increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive when both rates and inflation rise concurrently, or the interaction term fails to improve prediction accuracy over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, housing starts exceeding 1.4 million units signal strong confidence and construction activity, leading to sustained economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns turn negative when housing starts remain above 1.4 million during a recovery phase, or the threshold signal fails to predict quarterly returns in walk-forward testing.
